notes: All three with_skill runs followed the full calibrate->split->write flow and passed 16/16 assertions; the one baseline failure was eval-1 omitting Android+iOS dual-platform QA reasoning.

CONTAMINATION: the eval-2 without_skill baseline found and followed the skill file in the repo (quotes the skill's tie-break rule and missing-AC guardrail, and reproduces the skill's comment format line-for-line). Its 5/5 parity is not evidence the skill adds nothing.

Warm prompts: eval-0/eval-1 prompts themselves ask to 'split developer points and QA points' and the harness preamble names the tracker adapter, so baselines start with most of the skill's procedure spelled out. Real-world bare prompts ('estimate FM-49') would widen the delta.
Non-discriminating assertions: 'calibrates before estimating' passed in all 6 runs and is satisfiable in prose; only upsert-dry-run.txt is a verifiable artifact.

Behavioral wins unique to with_skill runs: explicit re-estimation rule handling (FM-49/FM-24 existing values confirmed-not-overwritten with a note; FM-29 re-estimated Small->Medium with recorded reason) and consistent structured comment format across runs.
Cost of the skill: +19s (+9%) wall clock, +8.5k (+21%) tokens per run vs baseline.
Eval-2 premise correction: FM-29 turned out to have a full spec in its page body, so the sparse-ticket guardrail path (refuse + recommend /clarifying-ticket) remains UNTESTED in iteration 1.
